name: Psilocybin
legalStatus: Illegal in most countries, decriminalized in some regions
commonNames: Magic Mushrooms, Shrooms, Psilocybe, Sacred Mushrooms
overview: Psilocybin is a naturally occurring psychedelic compound found in over 200 species of mushrooms, most commonly in the Psilocybe genus. It's considered one of the most well-researched and therapeutically promising psychedelics.
physicalEffects: Changes in heart rate and blood pressure
psychologicalEffects: Altered sense of time and space
contraindications: Heart conditions or high blood pressure

name: LSD
legalStatus: Schedule I in most countries
commonNames: Acid, Lucy, Tabs, Blotter, L, Doses
overview: LSD is a semisynthetic psychedelic derived from ergot alkaloids found in fungi. Discovered in 1938 and first experienced in 1943, it became the archetypal psychedelic and catalyst for the modern psychedelic movement.
physicalEffects: Increased heart rate and blood pressure
psychologicalEffects: Sense of cosmic or spiritual connection
Time distortion and temporal loop experiences
contraindications: Personal or family history of psychotic disorders
Use with lithium or other contraindicated medications

name: DMT
origin: Natural (found in plants, animals, and humans)
legalStatus: Schedule I in most countries
commonNames: DMT, Dimitri, Spirit Molecule, Businessman's Trip
overview: DMT is a powerful, naturally occurring psychedelic compound found in many plants and animals. Known for producing intense, short-duration experiences often described as "breakthrough" journeys to other dimensions.
physicalEffects: Increased heart rate and blood pressure
Minimal physical sensation awareness during peak
psychologicalEffects: Complete ego dissolution and identity loss
Experience of alternate dimensions or realities
benefits: Rapid ego dissolution for therapeutic insight
Treatment for depression and anxiety (research stage)
contraindications: History of psychosis or severe mental illness
Use with MAOIs or other medications

name: Mescaline
legalStatus: Schedule I in most countries, religious exemptions exist
commonNames: Mescaline, Peyote, San Pedro, Buttons, Cactus
overview: Mescaline is a naturally occurring psychedelic alkaloid found in several cacti species, most notably peyote and San Pedro. It has been used for thousands of years in religious and healing ceremonies by indigenous peoples of the Americas.
physicalEffects: Mild increases in heart rate and blood pressure
Generally gentle on the body compared to other psychedelics
psychologicalEffects: Sense of connection with nature and others
visualEffects: Geometric patterns and fractals (less intense than LSD)
Nature appearing more vivid and alive
Generally less visually intense than other psychedelics
benefits: Treatment for depression and anxiety (research stage)
Addiction treatment, especially alcohol use disorder
contraindications: Heart conditions or high blood pressure
Use with certain medications (MAOIs, some antidepressants)

name: 5-MeO-DMT
legalStatus: Schedule I in most countries
commonNames: 5-MeO-DMT, Bufo, Toad Venom, Five-methoxy, The God Molecule
overview: 5-MeO-DMT is one of the most potent naturally occurring psychedelics, found in various plants and the venom of the Colorado River toad. Known for producing intense ego dissolution experiences often described as "white light" or unity consciousness.
physicalEffects: Respiratory changes and possible breath holding
Rapid return to normal physical function
psychologicalEffects: Complete ego dissolution and identity loss
Experience of pure being or existence
Profound sense of home-coming or recognition
visualEffects: Intense white light or golden radiance
Geometric patterns (less common than other psychedelics)
Possible absence of any visual experience
Return to normal vision rapid and complete
benefits: Treatment for depression and existential anxiety
contraindications: History of psychotic disorders or severe mental illness

name: Ayahuasca
legalStatus: Illegal in most countries, religious exemptions exist
commonNames: Ayahuasca, Yagé, Hoasca, Daime, La Purga, Vine of the Soul
overview: Ayahuasca is a traditional plant medicine brew from the Amazon rainforest, combining the Banisteriopsis caapi vine with DMT-containing plants. Used for millennia by indigenous peoples for healing, divination, and spiritual communion.
physicalEffects: Increased heart rate and blood pressure
Deep relaxation alternating with physical discomfort
psychologicalEffects: Confrontation with personal shadows and traumas
visualEffects: Visions of animals, plants, and nature spirits
benefits: Treatment for depression, anxiety, and PTSD
Addiction recovery and substance abuse treatment
contraindications: History of psychotic disorders or severe mental illness
Use of SSRIs, MAOIs, or other contraindicated medications

name: Peyote
legalStatus: Illegal except for Native American Church religious use in US
commonNames: Peyote, Sacred Cactus, Hikuli, Buttons, Divine Cactus
overview: Peyote is a small, spineless cactus native to Mexico and southern Texas, containing the psychedelic compound mescaline. Sacred to many Native American tribes, it has been used for over 5,000 years in religious and healing ceremonies.
physicalEffects: Initial nausea and possible vomiting (considered purification)
Mild increases in heart rate and blood pressure
psychologicalEffects: Sense of communion with nature and spirits
Increased appreciation for life and relationships
visualEffects: Visions of animals, plants, and nature spirits
benefits: Enhanced connection with nature and environment
Treatment for depression and anxiety (traditional use)
contraindications: Use without proper cultural understanding or permission

name: San Pedro
legalStatus: Legal to grow in most places, mescaline extraction illegal
commonNames: San Pedro, Huachuma, Wachuma, Aguacolla, Gigantón
overview: San Pedro is a large columnar cactus native to the Andes mountains, containing the psychedelic compound mescaline. Used for over 3,000 years in traditional healing ceremonies, it offers a gentler and more nature-connected mescaline experience than peyote.
physicalEffects: Mild nausea during onset (less than peyote)
Enhanced physical sensitivity and body awareness
Slight increases in heart rate and blood pressure
Generally gentle and comfortable on the body
psychologicalEffects: Deep emotional healing and heart opening
Enhanced empathy and love for others
Sense of connection with nature and Mother Earth
visualEffects: Enhanced colors and natural beauty appreciation
Visions of nature, animals, and landscapes
Enhanced perception of natural textures and forms
benefits: Treatment for depression and anxiety (traditional use)
Enhanced connection with nature and environment
contraindications: Use of certain medications (MAOIs, some antidepressants)

name: 4-AcO-DMT
legalStatus: Unscheduled in many places, legal grey area
commonNames: 4-AcO-DMT, Psilacetin, O-Acetylpsilocin, Synthetic Shrooms
overview: 4-AcO-DMT is a synthetic psychedelic that is chemically related to psilocybin and converts to psilocin in the body. Often called "synthetic mushrooms," it produces effects very similar to psilocybin mushrooms but with more predictable dosing and purity.
physicalEffects: Possible nausea during onset (less than mushrooms)
Slight changes in heart rate and blood pressure
visualEffects: Enhancement of natural textures and patterns
benefits: More predictable dosing than natural mushrooms
Possible treatment for depression and anxiety

name: Iboga
legalStatus: Traditional use protected in Gabon, regulated elsewhere
commonNames: Iboga, Eboka, Bois Sacr�, Sacred Wood
overview: Iboga is a sacred shrub native to equatorial Africa, particularly Gabon, where it has been used for centuries by the Bwiti people in traditional healing and spiritual initiation ceremonies. The root bark contains multiple alkaloids including ibogaine, and represents one of Africa's most important plant medicines.
physicalEffects: Nausea and vomiting (considered part of purification)
Ataxia and loss of physical coordination
Possible cardiac effects with high doses
psychologicalEffects: Communication with ancestors and spiritual beings
Confrontation with personal and ancestral shadows
visualEffects: Visions of ancestors and spiritual guides
Scenes from personal and ancestral history
Encounters with animal spirits and totems
benefits: Connection with ancestral wisdom and culture
Healing of spiritual and psychological wounds
contraindications: Lack of cultural understanding or respect

name: Ibogaine
legalStatus: Illegal in most countries, legal for addiction treatment in some
commonNames: Ibogaine, Iboga Root, Bwiti Medicine
overview: Ibogaine is a naturally occurring psychoactive alkaloid found in the root bark of the African shrub Tabernanthe iboga. Known for its powerful anti-addictive properties, it's used in addiction treatment and spiritual healing, particularly in Bwiti traditions of Gabon.
physicalEffects: Severe nausea and vomiting (common and intense)
Possible dangerous interactions with heart conditions
psychologicalEffects: Intense life review and autobiographical memories
Confrontation with addiction patterns and behaviors
Possible encounters with spiritual entities or guides
visualEffects: Life review sequences and memory playback
Possible entity encounters and spiritual visions
Often described as "movies" of one's life
benefits: Powerful addiction interruption and recovery support
Treatment for opioid, cocaine, alcohol, and other addictions
Deep psychological healing and trauma processing
contraindications: Any heart conditions or cardiovascular disease

name: MDMA
legalStatus: Schedule I in most countries, breakthrough therapy designation for PTSD
commonNames: Ecstasy, Molly, E, X, Adam, Love Drug
overview: MDMA is a synthetic compound that produces both stimulant and empathogenic effects. Often called the "love drug," it's gained recognition as a breakthrough therapy for PTSD and is nearing FDA approval for therapeutic use.
physicalEffects: Elevated heart rate and blood pressure
psychologicalEffects: Intense feelings of love and empathy
Heightened appreciation for music and touch
visualEffects: Generally minimal compared to classic psychedelics
benefits: Possible applications for depression and anxiety
contraindications: History of stroke or cerebrovascular issues
Use with MAOIs or other contraindicated medications

name: Salvia Divinorum
legalStatus: Varies by jurisdiction, legal in many places
commonNames: Salvia, Diviner's Sage, Ska María Pastora, Magic Mint, Sally-D
overview: Salvia divinorum is a plant in the mint family native to the cloud forests of Mexico, containing the unique psychoactive compound salvinorin A. Used traditionally by Mazatec shamans for divination and healing, it produces intense but brief dissociative experiences unlike any other psychedelic.
physicalEffects: Profound loss of motor control and coordination
Rapid return to normal physical function
psychologicalEffects: Transformation into objects, places, or other beings
Feeling of being pulled or dragged by forces
Often described as bizarre and alien
visualEffects: Transformation of environment into completely different reality
Often described as more real than real
benefits: Traditional use for divination and spiritual insight
No known physical toxicity or addiction potential
contraindications: History of psychotic disorders or reality dissociation issues
Any situation requiring motor control or awareness
Use without proper supervision and safety measures

name: 2C-B
legalStatus: Schedule I in most countries
commonNames: 2C-B, Nexus, Venus, Bees, Tootsie, Bromo
overview: 2C-B is a synthetic psychedelic first synthesized by Alexander Shulgin in 1974. It combines visual psychedelic effects with empathogenic qualities, making it unique among psychedelics for its dual nature and relatively gentle experience profile.
physicalEffects: Mild increases in heart rate and blood pressure
Enhanced sensitivity to touch and temperature
visualEffects: Enhanced visual acuity and depth perception
Generally more colorful and playful than other psychedelics
benefits: Possible applications for social anxiety treatment
contraindications: Use with certain medications (MAOIs, some antidepressants)

name: Ketamine
legalStatus: Schedule III (controlled but legal for medical use)
commonNames: Ketamine, Special K, K, Ket, Vitamin K, Cat Tranquilizer
overview: Ketamine is a dissociative anesthetic that has gained recognition as a breakthrough treatment for depression. Originally developed for medical anesthesia, it's now used both therapeutically for mental health conditions and recreationally for its unique consciousness-altering effects.
physicalEffects: Profound motor impairment and loss of coordination
Numbness and loss of physical sensation
Increased heart rate and blood pressure
Nausea and vomiting (especially at higher doses)
Potential respiratory depression at high doses
psychologicalEffects: Ego dissolution and loss of identity
Altered perception of time and space
Possible antidepressant effects lasting days to weeks
visualEffects: Possible complete visual replacement at high doses
contraindications: Severe cardiovascular disease or uncontrolled hypertension

name: Cannabis
legalStatus: Rapidly changing, legal in many jurisdictions
commonNames: Cannabis, Marijuana, Weed, Ganja, Herb, Mary Jane, Pot
overview: Cannabis is one of the most widely used psychoactive plants in the world, containing compounds like THC and CBD. While typically classified as a mild psychedelic, at higher doses it can produce profound altered states of consciousness, visual effects, and spiritual experiences.
visualEffects: Enhanced color perception and visual acuity
Possible visual distortions at higher doses
Enhanced appreciation for visual art and beauty
benefits: Treatment for chronic pain and inflammation
Enhancement of creativity and artistic expression
Possible therapeutic applications for PTSD and trauma
contraindications: History of psychotic disorders or schizophrenia
Severe anxiety disorders (though may help some)
Use before driving or operating machinery

name: Datura
legalStatus: Legal to grow in most places, dangerous to consume
commonNames: Datura, Jimsonweed, Devil's Trumpet, Thorn Apple, Mad Hatter
overview: Datura is a highly toxic plant containing tropane alkaloids that produce powerful and dangerous deliriant effects. Unlike classical psychedelics, datura creates realistic but terrifying hallucinations and is associated with serious medical risks and psychological trauma.
physicalEffects: Extreme pupil dilation and vision problems
Severe dry mouth and inability to swallow
Dangerous increases in heart rate and blood pressure
Possible seizures, coma, and respiratory failure
Complete loss of motor control and coordination
psychologicalEffects: Complete delirium and loss of reality testing
Conversations with people who aren't there
visualEffects: Extremely realistic hallucinations indistinguishable from reality
Spiders, insects, and other disturbing imagery common
Users cannot tell what is real vs. hallucinated
benefits: Historical use in traditional medicine (highly toxic)
Some compounds used in modern medicine in controlled doses
Research applications under strict medical supervision
contraindications: Everyone - no safe recreational dose exists
Particularly dangerous for those with heart conditions
Extremely dangerous for those with mental health conditions

name: MDA
legalStatus: Schedule I in most countries
commonNames: MDA, Sally, Love Drug, Tenamfetamine
overview: MDA is a psychedelic amphetamine that combines empathogenic effects with visual psychedelic properties. Often called the "love drug," it predates MDMA and offers a more psychedelic experience with enhanced visual effects and introspective qualities.
physicalEffects: Enhanced tactile sensations and touch sensitivity
Increased heart rate and blood pressure
Possible jaw clenching and muscle tension
visualEffects: Enhanced visual acuity and depth perception
More prominent visual effects than MDMA
benefits: Possible treatment for depression and anxiety
contraindications: History of heart conditions or cardiovascular disease
Use with certain medications (MAOIs, some antidepressants)

name: DOC
legalStatus: Schedule I in most countries
commonNames: DOC, 2,5-Dimethoxy-4-chloroamphetamine
overview: DOC is a synthetic psychedelic amphetamine created by Alexander Shulgin, known for its extremely long duration and intense effects. Part of the DOx family, it combines stimulant properties with powerful psychedelic visuals and can last up to 24 hours.
physicalEffects: Increased heart rate and blood pressure
Possible muscle tension and jaw clenching
visualEffects: Extremely intense and complex geometric patterns
Among the most intense visuals of any psychedelic
contraindications: History of heart conditions or cardiovascular disease
Use with stimulant medications or substances

name: Muscimol
legalStatus: Legal in most jurisdictions
commonNames: Amanita muscaria, Fly Agaric, Fly Amanita, Red and White Mushroom
overview: Muscimol is the primary psychoactive compound found in Amanita muscaria (fly agaric) mushrooms. Unlike psilocybin, muscimol acts on GABA receptors, producing dream-like states, altered perception, and unique experiences that differ significantly from classical psychedelics.
physicalEffects: Altered perception of body size and weight
Possible nausea (less common than with psilocybin)
psychologicalEffects: Altered perception of time and space
Enhanced connection with nature and folklore
visualEffects: Generally less intense visuals than classical psychedelics
benefits: Enhanced dream work and lucid dreaming
Possible applications for anxiety and insomnia
Connection with folklore and traditional practices
contraindications: History of severe mental health conditions
Use with alcohol or other depressants

name: AL-LAD
legalStatus: Legal grey area in many jurisdictions
commonNames: AL-LAD, Aladdin, 6-Allyl-6-nor-LSD
overview: AL-LAD is a synthetic psychedelic closely related to LSD, known for producing visual-heavy experiences with less intense psychological effects. Often described as "LSD-lite," it offers colorful, playful visuals with reduced anxiety and more manageable headspace compared to LSD.
physicalEffects: Slight changes in heart rate and blood pressure
Minimal body load compared to LSD
visualEffects: Enhanced visual acuity and depth perception
Often described as most visual lysergamide
benefits: Personal growth with reduced psychological intensity
Introduction to psychedelics with gentler profile

name: 25I-NBOMe
legalStatus: Schedule I in most countries
commonNames: 25I-NBOMe, N-Bomb, Smiles, 25I, 2C-I-NBOMe
overview: 25I-NBOMe is a highly potent synthetic psychedelic from the NBOMe family, known for intense visual effects but carrying significant safety risks. Often sold as "fake LSD," it has caused numerous hospitalizations and deaths due to its toxicity and unpredictable effects.
physicalEffects: Dangerous increases in heart rate and blood pressure
Possible cardiac arrest and respiratory failure
psychologicalEffects: Intense and often overwhelming visual hallucinations
visualEffects: Often described as too intense to be enjoyable
benefits: Research applications only under strict medical supervision
Academic interest in receptor binding studies
contraindications: Particularly dangerous for those with heart conditions
Extremely dangerous for those with mental health conditions
Should not be used by anyone outside research settings

name: DPT
legalStatus: Schedule I in most countries
commonNames: DPT, Dipropyltryptamine, The Light
overview: DPT is a synthetic tryptamine psychedelic known for producing intense, often challenging experiences with deep introspective and spiritual qualities. Used historically in psychedelic therapy research, it tends to produce more serious and contemplative experiences than DMT.
physicalEffects: Initial anxiety and body tension common
Increased heart rate and blood pressure
psychologicalEffects: Confrontation with personal shadows and traumas
Possible encounters with death and mortality themes
visualEffects: Closed-eye visuals often more prominent than open-eye
benefits: Deep psychological healing and trauma processing
Treatment for depression and existential anxiety
contraindications: Use without proper preparation and support

name: 4-HO-MET
legalStatus: Legal grey area in many jurisdictions
commonNames: 4-HO-MET, Metocin, Color
overview: 4-HO-MET is a synthetic psilocybin analogue known for producing colorful, visual-heavy experiences with minimal psychological content. Often described as "recreational psilocybin," it offers beautiful visuals and euphoria with reduced introspective intensity and anxiety.
physicalEffects: Generally comfortable and pleasant physical sensations
Minimal nausea compared to other tryptamines
visualEffects: Enhanced visual acuity and depth perception
Often described as most visually beautiful tryptamine
benefits: Introduction to psychedelics with gentle profile
Reduced anxiety compared to other psychedelics

name: PCP
legalStatus: Schedule II (medical use) / Schedule I (recreational)
commonNames: PCP, Angel Dust, Phencyclidine, Peace Pill, Hog
overview: PCP is a dissociative anesthetic that produces powerful dissociative effects and altered states of consciousness. Originally developed as an anesthetic, it's known for unpredictable and potentially dangerous effects including dissociation, anesthesia, and possible aggressive behavior.
physicalEffects: Complete anesthesia and loss of pain sensation
Dangerous loss of motor control and coordination
Increased heart rate and blood pressure
Risk of injury due to anesthesia and coordination loss
psychologicalEffects: Profound dissociation from reality and identity
visualEffects: Often less prominent than psychological effects
benefits: Medical anesthetic use only under strict supervision
Research applications in controlled medical settings
contraindications: Particularly dangerous for those with mental health conditions
Extremely dangerous for those with heart conditions
Should only be used in medical settings by professionals

name: 3-MeO-PCP
legalStatus: Legal grey area in many jurisdictions
commonNames: 3-MeO-PCP, 3-Methoxy-PCP
overview: 3-MeO-PCP is a synthetic dissociative drug related to PCP but with a more manageable and less dangerous profile. Known for producing clear-headed dissociation, enhanced creativity, and therapeutic-like effects at appropriate doses, though it still carries significant risks.
physicalEffects: Loss of motor coordination and balance
Generally less physical discomfort than PCP
visualEffects: Possible geometric patterns at higher doses
Generally less prominent than psychological effects
benefits: Potential therapeutic applications for depression and anxiety
Possible applications in therapy and personal growth
Less dangerous than PCP while maintaining dissociative benefits
contraindications: Use before driving or operating machinery

name: Nitrous Oxide
legalStatus: Legal for medical/commercial use, recreational use varies
commonNames: Nitrous Oxide, Laughing Gas, Nos, Whippits, Nangs
overview: Nitrous oxide is a dissociative gas commonly known as "laughing gas," used medically as an anesthetic and recreationally for its brief euphoric and dissociative effects. It produces short-lived altered consciousness, laughter, and possible mystical experiences.
physicalEffects: Temporary loss of motor control and coordination
psychologicalEffects: Possible feelings of floating or weightlessness
Enhanced humor and tendency to laugh
visualEffects: Possible tunnel vision or visual changes
benefits: Brief stress relief and mood enhancement
Quick recovery with no lasting impairment
contraindications: Use in confined spaces or while driving

name: 1P-LSD
legalStatus: Legal grey area in many jurisdictions
commonNames: 1P-LSD, 1-Propionyl-LSD
overview: 1P-LSD is a psychedelic lysergamide that acts as a prodrug to LSD, producing nearly identical effects. Often used as a legal alternative to LSD, it's believed to convert to LSD in the body, making it functionally equivalent to LSD in terms of experience and effects.
physicalEffects: Slight changes in heart rate and blood pressure
visualEffects: Enhanced visual acuity and depth perception

name: Changa
legalStatus: Illegal in most countries (due to DMT content)
commonNames: Changa, Enhanced Leaf, Smokable Ayahuasca
overview: Changa is a smokable herbal blend containing DMT and MAOI-containing plants, creating a more gradual and extended DMT experience. Often called "smokable ayahuasca," it combines the visual intensity of DMT with the extended duration and deeper integration time of ayahuasca.
physicalEffects: Generally gentler onset than pure DMT
Enhanced connection with body and environment
psychologicalEffects: Access to archetypal and symbolic material
More integrated experience than pure DMT
visualEffects: Vivid colors and enhanced visual depth
Often described as more coherent than pure DMT
benefits: Connection with nature and universal consciousness
More accessible than ayahuasca for many users
contraindications: Use of SSRIs, MAOIs, or other contraindicated medications

name: Harmaline
classification: Beta-carboline MAOI with Mild Psychoactive Properties
origin: Natural (found in Banisteriopsis caapi, Peganum harmala, others)
legalStatus: Legal in most jurisdictions as isolated compound
commonNames: Harmaline, Telepathine, Banisterine, Yageine
overview: Harmaline is a beta-carboline alkaloid found in various plants including Banisteriopsis caapi and Peganum harmala. As an MAOI and mild psychoactive compound, it enables oral DMT activity and produces its own unique consciousness-altering effects including enhanced dreams and spiritual experiences.
physicalEffects: Generally comfortable and gentle physical experience
psychologicalEffects: Enhanced connection with nature and environment
visualEffects: Mild enhancement of colors and visual depth
Generally minimal visual effects compared to classical psychedelics
benefits: Enhanced dream states and dream recall
Traditional use for healing and spiritual growth
MAOI activity for combination with tryptamines
contraindications: Use of SSRIs, MAOIs, or other contraindicated medications

name: LSA
origin: Natural (morning glory and Hawaiian baby woodrose seeds)
legalStatus: Seeds legal in most places, LSA compound scheduling varies
commonNames: LSA, Ergine, Morning Glory, Hawaiian Baby Woodrose, HBWR
overview: LSA is a naturally occurring psychedelic compound found in morning glory seeds and Hawaiian baby woodrose seeds. As a precursor to LSD, it produces similar but generally milder effects with more body load and nausea, offering a natural lysergamide experience.
physicalEffects: Heavy body load and physical discomfort
Possible stomach pain and gastrointestinal distress
psychologicalEffects: Generally milder psychological effects than LSD
Possible anxiety related to physical discomfort
visualEffects: Generally less intense visuals than LSD
Visual effects often overshadowed by body load
